texts.full_text: ============ Atrial fibrillation (AF) is a major public health problem and the most common cardiac arrhythmia encountered in clinical practice. In 2017, the estimated prevalence ranged from 2.7 million to 6.1 million in the United States and is projected to grow to 12 million by 2030.^[@r1]^ In addition, AF is responsible for approximately 100,000 deaths^[@r2]^ and 500,000 hospitalizations annually^[@r2],[@r3]^ and is associated with a fivefold increased risk of stroke,^[@r4]--[@r7]^ a threefold increased risk of heart failure,^[@r8]--[@r10]^ and an increase in all-cause mortality.^[@r5],[@r11]--[@r13]^ The burden of AF is currently epidemic and anticipated to grow in prevalence due to an aging population that is living longer with multiple comorbid conditions associated with the development of AF.^[@r14],[@r15]^ Moreover, AF has been linked with numerous systematic and non-systematic conditions that contribute to its development and progression, including hypertension, diabetes, obesity, and sleep apnea.^[@r16],[@r17]^ The economic burden of AF is also significant, with annual costs in the United States estimated to range from $6 to $11 billion, including costs of hospitalization, medical care, and lost productivity.^[@r18]^ Given the increasing burden of AF, there is a critical need for improved strategies in the prevention, diagnosis, and management of the condition to reduce the associated morbidity and mortality.^[@r19]^ This review aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of AF, including its epidemiology, risk factors, and the strategies for its management and prevention, to inform clinical practice and pu

texts.full_text: se Print Products Human Kinetics print books are now distributed by Footprint Books throughout Australia/NZ, delivered to you from their NSW warehouse. Please visit Footprint Books to order your Human Kinetics print products. # Sport and Exercise Psychology: A Critical Introduction (Paperback) ## Description The book provides a critical introduction for students in sport psychology that challenges the dominant approaches taken by many textbooks. Sport psychologists are often criticised as being too ‘scientific’ or not scientific enough. This is because there has been an over-reliance on quantitative research, particularly laboratory-based studies with university undergraduates and elite athletes to inform our understanding of how people behave when they take part in sport-related activities (e.g., exercise). In this book the author challenges these dominant approaches by providing a critical introduction for students. He argues that it is important we question what counts as scientific knowledge, who decides on such matters and why? The aim throughout Sport Psychology: A Critical Introduction has been to help readers understand how sport psychology can be used in applied settings (e.g., health promotion) while also considering the limits of this approach. This book will appeal most strongly to students studying a module or course that includes critical approaches, for example: • An introduction and/or overview into Sport Psychology • A Critical Introduction to Sports Studies / PE & Society modules ## Details

This study complied with this recommendation and therefore the estimate of ESDs for the various examinations could be considered sufficiently representative value for the specific rooms. This study has provided some initial base line data on the size of the average adult patient in Ghana and the corresponding dose for pelvis examinations. Description of the Prior Art Active, or capacitive elements can be incorporated into connectors to reduce cross-talk, improve impedance matching, filter out EMI/RFI or radio-frequency interference), suppress transients (voltage “spikes”), and for various other purposes. Such elements are sometimes known as “chip capacitors” or simply and will be so referred here. The chips may be placed across two signal lines, from signal lines to ground, or otherwise as appropriate. The chips may be held in a hole in the insulating housing of the connector, which provides both location and protection for the chip. This is illustrated by U.S. Pat. No. 6,036,545 to Caviness et a1l., where an interference suppression capacitor (chip) sits across two signal lines in each contact pair of one row or bank; see FIGs: 2A-B and col.: 7 line : 8 —9. Alternatively the chip may be placed on lands formed integrally but extending from a wall surface at least partially defining an interior chamber within which electrical contacts are disposed when assembled in mating condition to form signal paths for conveying data signals therebetween; see U: S 5 ,4316720 (Kuo) and US Pat No.: 8A. Fifty-five articles were included, containing 3,945 large incisional hernia repairs ([@B2]). "Mesh reinforced techniques displayed better recurrence rates and hazards than techniques without mesh reinforcement, including component separation technique without mesh" ([@B2]). Of all the mesh techniques, including componentseparation with mesh reinforcement, the use of synthetic or biologic meshes has been shown to significantly reduce the risk of hernia recurrence. However, the choice between synthetic and biologic meshes depends on several factors, such as patient comorbidities, infection risk, and surgeon preference ([@B3]). Synthetic meshes, typically made from materials like polypropylene, polyester, or PTFE (polytetrafluoroethylene), offer robust mechanical support but carry a higher risk of infection if contaminated. Biologic meshes, derived from human or animal tissues that are decellularized to reduce immunogenicity, are more suitable for patients at high risk of infection or in contaminated fields due to their ability to be incorporated into the host tissue and remodel over time ([@B4]). The component separation technique (CST) combined with mesh reinforcement has emerged as a preferred method for large incisional hernia repair. CST involves releasing the internal oblique and transversus abdominis muscles to expand the native fascial area, allowing for tension-free closure of the defect and better distribution of abdominal wall tension ([@B5]). texts.full_text: er underwent ultrasonography with this probe. The tumors were staged using the TNM system, and the results were compared with the histologic findings of the resected specimens. The FLUP probes provided high-resolution images that allowed accurate delineation between tumor tissue (T) layers in 35 patients; however, the probe was not able to pass through all stenotic areas, and thus staging could be limited by this factor. In conclusion: The use of a front-loading ultrasound transducer for preoperative rectal cancer evaluation is feasible. The high-resolution images obtained with the FLUP allowed accurate delineation between tumor tissue layers in most patients; however, the probe's inability to pass through tight stenosis may limit its application, particularly when staging involves more advanced disease. The study demonstrated that this novel imaging technique could be a valuable tool for preoperative evaluation of rectal cancer. texts.full_text: ond officer, who along with the rest of the crew and some passengers, handled the crisis without the airplane ever being in jeopardy. (Second officers are usually flying the airplane most of the time anyway.) Accounts from this episode portray a total emotional breakdown but not a suicidal urge to take down the flight. The starkest case of a pilot deliberately plunging his airplane to disaster was that of EgyptAir flight 990 in October 1999. In this instance a relief first officer was flying a Boeing 767 from JFK to Cairo and was about 60 miles out over the Atlantic when he throttled back the engines and pushed the airplane into a nosedive. The cockpit voice recorder, recovered from the ocean, revealed that the first officer muttered “Tawkalt ala Allah” (“I rely on God”) several times as he made his moves. The captain, rushing back to the flight deck, is heard yelling ,“What’s happening? What’s happening?” But by then it was too late to recover, and all 217 on board were killed. The record of the first officer’s voice, and the fact that National Transportation Safety Board investigators found no evidence of any mechanical failure, made it clear that—for some inexplicable reason—the first officer had decided to commit suicide and take everyone else with him. Recipients provide a blog or Q&A after the event, which is cool because you get your own press! Here's a secret: often when we open applications for specific events, we don't get that many applicants because people assume someone else is in more need than they are -- and that is really silly. Let US decide who not only has the greatest need, but would make the best use of the opportunity for their own career path. Yeah, it's actually free. No, it's not too good to be true. We aren't predatory. We just want to do the right thing because we've been in situations where we've missed out. texts.full_text: form of up­right locomo­tion when mov­ing through trees. They do this by grasp­ing branches with their hands and feet to main­tain bal­ance, which sug­gests an al­terna­tive ori­gin for bi­pe­dalism. This obser­va­tion leads re­s­earchers to pro­pose that up­right walk­ing may have evolved as a way of mov­ing through the cano­py in search of food, rather than on open ground. This would mean our ances­tors could already be ca­pa­ble of bi­pe­dalism while still liv­ing pri­mar­i­ly in trees. The find­ings sug­gest that up­right walk­ing may have been a more com­mon trait among ear­li­er apes than pre­vi­ously thought, and it could have evolved mul­tiple times as dif­ferent spe­cies adapted to their en­viron­ments. This chal­lenges the tra­di­tion­al view that bi­pe­dalism is a unique trait of hu­man ances­tors. Speedy J, with his long-standing influence on the genre, brings a wealth of experience and a penchant for experimentation, while Lucy, with her fresh and dynamic approach, brings a new perspective and energy. Together, they create a project that doesn't just push the boundaries of techno, but redefines them. The album, a masterpiece of sound design and composition, stands out for its intricate layers and textures. It's a journey through soundscapes that are both and entirely new, a dance of contrasts and harmonies that challenge the listener's expectations. A: I think you could consider 3 options: 1) make your graph huge and hope your labels do not overlap 2) plot the points as different series with each itting a unique label for every point, which will be displayed on separate lines in gnuplot's output. texts.full_text: d A Card Peter Thomes December 5, 1959 — January 26, 2021 Pete Thomes, 61, of Brainerd, Minnesota died Tuesday, January 26, 2021 after a courageous battle with cancer. Visitation for Pete will be held for one hour prior to the service to be held at 2:00 p.m. on Saturday, January 30 at Heritage Church, Baxter. Pete was born in Buffalo, MN on December 5, 1959, to Harold and Marlene Thomes. He grew up in Baxter, Minnesota, and was a proud graduate of Brainerd High School, class of 1977. Pete married the love of his life, Cindy, on August 10, 1980, and they shared 40 wonderful years of marriage. Together, they raised two children, Adam and Allison, who were the light of his life. Pete was a hardworking man, dedicating his career to serving his community as a dedicated paramedic. He was a family man first and foremost, cherishing every moment spent with his loved ones. Pete was known for his kindness, his infectious smile, and his unwavering optimism. He was a loyal friend and a dedicated family man who will be deeply missed by all who knew him. Is it true that $f$ is strictly increasing on $\mathbb R$ if and only if $f'(x) \geq 0$ on $\mathbb R$ and the set $X = \{x \in \mathbb{R} \,| \, f'(x) = 0\}$ is countable? Suppose $f$ is differentiable and strictly increasing. Then yes, we must have $f'\ge 0$ everywhere. However, we can have $f'=0$ on an uncountable set, in fact a set of positive measure. Example: Let $U$ be an open set containing the rationals, with $m(U)<1.$ Let $E=\mathbb R\setminus U.$ Then $E$ is closed. Define $$f(x)=\int_0^x d(t,E)\ dt,$$ where $d(t,E)$ denotes the distance from $t$ to $E.$ Then $f'(x)=0$ only on $U$ (the rationals), and this set is uncountable. texts.full_text: that these barriers remain firmly adhered to both intact and non-intact skin surfaces. One of the key challenges with current topical skin barriers is their opacity after application. This lack of transparency makes it difficult to visually assess the condition of the underlying skin without aggressively removing portions of the barrier. Such aggressive removal can further exacerbate skin damage, highlighting the need for a more transparent and durable solution that allows for non-invasive inspection. In summary, topical skin barriers are vital in preventing skin breakdown caused by moisture and waste exposure. Ensuring these barriers remain adhered to both intact and non-intact skin is critical for their effectiveness. Additionally, addressing the opacity issue will help prevent unnecessary skin damage during visual inspections. While any topical skin barrier that protects skin from exposure to moisture and waste, acting as a barrier therefrom, may be beneficial, an ability to remain adhered to both intact and non-intact skin is obviously critical to satisfactory performance. Furthermore, known topical skin barriers are often substantially opaque after application to skin which does not allow the condition of the skin to be visually assessed. texts.full_text: 2018 Nick3 Comments This post is a continuation of the story of Luella Johnston, Sacramento (and California’s) first elected councilwoman. I strongly recommend starting with Part I, where I discussed Luella’s civic and political activism in Sacramento, culminating in her successful 1912 campaign for a seat on the Sacramento City Council. In Part II, I discuss her first year on the City Council. Tacoma Times (1912) Rallying behind Luella’s candidacy, Sacramento’s women helped sweep local Progressives to victory in the 1912 city elections. It was a landmark year for Sacramento’s reformers, who had finally succeeded in kicking out the political machine that had long dominated city politics. Now came the challenge of governing. The New Government: One of Business & Efficiency Official City Seal (1912) 1912 was unlike any prior year for another reason: The five freshman councilmembers would be the first to serve under the new city charter. Prior to 1912, Sacramento had a traditional “Str...straw poll system where councillors were elected by their wards and often controlled by party machines. In contrast, Luella Johnston's Progressive approach stressed efficiency, business-like government practices, and direct citizen involvement through council meetings open to the public. Therefore, through group consensus, CHIME developed the idea of a “Service Team.” Leadership roles are clearly defined and broken down into “mother sized” jobs. Members of the Service Team are asked to serve for one year or two years. Those serving two years act to provide for a smooth transition from year to year. Service Team members may choose to serve an additional year if the majority of CHIME members re-elect them at the annual meeting. The Service Team meets twice per year, once in August to discuss any issues for the coming academic scholatic year and once in January to evaluate the fall activities and plan the Spring events. texts.full_text: potential (CAP) were studied by means of electrodes chronically implanted near the round window in 15 pigmented guinea pigs aged 3-6 months. The potentials were recorded before, during and after exposure to continuous pink noise maintained at an intensity of 80, 90 or 100 dB SPL for 120 h. During the exposureto moderate-intensity noises (MNI), CAP thresholds increased significantly in all animals tested with both stimuli used; a low-frequency click train and clicks delivered every second to mimic human speech patterns. Upon cessation of MNI, there was no significant change from baseline values for either stimulus. However, after 2 weeks following the end of exposure (post-exposure period), CAP thresholds had returned towards their pre-MNI levels in most animals exposed at both intensities tested with low-frequency click trains and single clicks. These results suggest that MNI can cause a reversible increase in auditory threshold for certain acoustic stimuli, which may be related to temporary changes within the cochlea or central processing pathways.
